title: glechoma calvertii
intro: Glechoma calvertii, also known as Calvert's mint or American gill, is a perennial plant native to North America, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and characterized by its fragrant, blue-purple flowers and square stems. The medicinal properties of Glechoma calvertii include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to relieve respiratory issues, reduce fever, and soothe digestive problems, as well as its antioxidant properties which may help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Herbal preparations of Glechoma calvertii can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
